name: Venezuela: OFAC sanctions and humanitarian general licenses (GL 4C, GL 26, GL 29)
access_mechanism_type: Regulatory framework shaping legal access pathways. The OFAC Venezuela Sanctions Regulations (31 CFR Part 591, since 2017 EO 13808 et seq.) bar most US-person dealings with the Government of Venezuela. Three principal humanitarian general licenses carve out medical/pharmaceutical activity: (1) GL 4C - exports of agricultural commodities, medicine, medical devices, replacement parts; (2) GL 26 - emergency medical services; (3) GL 29 - NGO humanitarian projects. These licenses authorize pharmaceutical importers, NGOs, and medical service providers to operate without specific OFAC license.
procurement_channels: Affects channels in this manner: (1) US-origin pharmaceutical exports to Venezuela (including via third-country resellers) authorized under GL 4C. (2) NGO drug donations and humanitarian operations (Convite, Codevida, MSF, Caritas) authorized under GL 29. (3) Emergency medical services involving the Government of Venezuela (e.g., MPPS hospital staff dealing with US-supplied equipment) authorized under GL 26. (4) Banking compliance friction creates de-risking - many US banks decline VE-related transactions despite licenses, raising costs and delays.
key_actors: OFAC (US Department of the Treasury Office of Foreign Assets Control) - issues and updates licenses. US State Department - sanctions policy. NGOs operating under GL 29: MSF-USA, Catholic Relief Services, Direct Relief, Project HOPE. US pharmaceutical exporters operating under GL 4C. Venezuelan government entities (MPPS, INHRR, SACS) - counterparty subject to sanctions but excepted under humanitarian licenses.
formulary_coverage: Not directly applicable; GL 4C covers all medicines and medical devices regardless of Venezuelan formulary status.
registration_authority: OFAC, US Department of the Treasury. Legal basis: International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA), Executive Orders 13692 (2015), 13808 (2017), 13835, 13850, 13857, 13884 (2019). Implementing regulations: 31 CFR Part 591 (Venezuela Sanctions Regulations).
reform_status: GL 4C issued August 5, 2019 (superseded GL 4B); remains in effect with no expiration date. GL 26 issued August 5, 2019; remains in effect. GL 29 issued August 5, 2019; remains in effect. Subsequent reforms 2023-2024: GL 44 (October 18, 2023) and GL 44A (April 17, 2024) authorized broader oil/gas transactions; later partially wound down. GL 41A and GL 5R (July 23, 2025). Humanitarian framework (GL 4C/26/29) untouched by these adjustments. Reform_status: enacted_stable for humanitarian licenses.
currency_mechanism: Sanctions complicate USD payment channels. US banks frequently de-risk Venezuelan transactions even when GL-authorized. Many manufacturers price in EUR via European subsidiaries to circumvent USD banking issues.
pharma_import_cost_impact: Significant indirect cost. Compliance overhead (KYC, sanctions screening, payment routing) raises pharmaceutical import costs estimated 5-15% above benchmark. [uncertain: precise impact figure] Some manufacturers withdrew from Venezuelan market not due to sanctions per se but due to compliance/payment friction. UN Special Rapporteur Alena Douhan (2021) and subsequent reports argued sanctions contributed to humanitarian impact despite carve-outs.
financial_mechanism: Not applicable directly; GLs enable but do not finance.
payer_debt_crisis: Sanctions complicate Venezuelan government repayment to international suppliers (frozen assets, blocked accounts), exacerbating arrears.
shortage_rate: Sanctions are a contributing factor to shortages (per UN reports) but not the primary cause - the primary causes are domestic budget collapse and currency controls. GLs were designed specifically to prevent humanitarian impact.
shortage_causes: Indirect contribution via banking de-risking and compliance friction. UN Special Rapporteur Alena Douhan (2021 report) concluded US sanctions impacted access to medicine despite humanitarian carve-outs. Counterargument: HRW and others argue Venezuelan health crisis predates and exceeds sanction effects.
shortage_monitoring_system: OFAC maintains licensing FAQs (665 et seq.) clarifying medical/humanitarian permissions. No formal mechanism to track license utilization.
oncology_specific_shortage_events: [uncertain: specific oncology-specific sanctions-related events] Some specialty pharmaceutical companies have publicly cited compliance friction as reason for limited Venezuelan supply.
source_citation: 1) OFAC, 'Venezuela-Related Sanctions', https://ofac.treasury.gov/sanctions-programs-and-country-information/venezuela-related-sanctions. 2) OFAC, 'Selected General Licenses Issued by OFAC', https://ofac.treasury.gov/selected-general-licenses-issued-ofac. 3) OFAC, 'Venezuela Sanctions FAQs' (FAQ 1581 series), https://ofac.treasury.gov/faqs/topic/1581. 4) Sanctions.org, 'OFAC - Venezuela General License 26 - Emergency and Certain Other Medical Services Authorized', https://sanctions.org/turbofac/research/OFAC-Venezuela-General-License-26. 5) Federal Register 88 FR 1875 (Jan 11, 2023), 'Publication of Venezuela Sanctions Regulations Web General Licenses 1, 2, 4, and Subsequent Iterations'. 6) Federal Register 89 FR 35345 (May 2, 2024), 'Publication of Venezuela Sanctions Regulations Web General Licenses 5O, 8M, and 44A'. 7) Federal Register 90 FR 33887 (July 23, 2025), 'GL 41A, 5R, 41B'. 8) Locke Lord QuickStudy, 'OFAC Issues Venezuela-Related General Licenses and FAQs' (Troutman Pepper Locke). 9) UN OHCHR, 'Report of the Special Rapporteur on the negative impact of unilateral coercive measures on the enjoyment of human rights, Alena Douhan' (A/HRC/48/59, 2021).
source_type_granular: government_primary (OFAC/Federal Register) + multilateral_PAHO_WHO (UN OHCHR) + legal_regulatory_tracker (Locke Lord, Sanctions.org)
data_reliability: high - OFAC licenses and Federal Register notices are primary government documents
kb_action: new_fact_add_to_doc - confirm GL 4C/26/29 issued August 5, 2019; no expiration; document stable humanitarian carve-out framework alongside Venezuelan procurement system narrative
